stories.title: Following career year, Sabres’ Peyton Krebs files for salary arbitration
stories.summary:
Buffalo Sabres forward Peyton Krebs has filed for salary arbitration, the NHL Players’ Association announced Sunday.
Krebs, 25, is the Sabres’ only unsigned restricted free agent.
Filing, of course, doesn’t mean a player will go to arbitration. Negotiations can continue. The sides usually reach a deal before the arbitration hearing.

Players who file for arbitration want more than their qualifying offer would pay them.
Fifteen players filed for arbitration, according to the NHLPA.
The deadline ... Read the full article 
The post Following career year, Sabres’ Peyton Krebs files for salary arbitration first appeared on Buffalo Hockey Beat.

stories.title: Bills holding open practice Aug 18 - tickets will be open to the public
stories.summary:
Tickets will be available to general public.
	 



	“Return of the Blue and Red, an annual intrasquad scrimmage held by the Buffalo Bills in Orchard Park, will not be the only opportunity for fans to experience the new Highmark Stadium on a non-game day in August.



	Pete Guelli, the Bills' president of business operations, told The Buffalo News that first-year coach Joe Brady will hold an open practice in the $2.1 billion, 60,108-seat stadium on the morning of Tuesday, Aug. 18.



	Holding a personal seat license doesn’t get you first dibs to the practice, either. Details will be announced soon, Guelli added, but tickets are expected to be made available online by the Bills on July 21. Everyone has the same odds of attending the open practice.”



	 



	https://buffalonews.com/sports/professional/nfl/bills/article_b9979b83-2d5d-43f9-b4f5-d6b9bf730b7f.html?utm_campaign=snd-autopilot&utm_medium=social&utm_source=facebook_The_Buffalo_News&fbclid=IwdGRleAS3stdleHRuA2FlbQIxMQBzcnRjBmFwcF9pZAo2NjI4NTY4Mzc5AAEeWn7PFQS-4whqoYG2La7oX9OQo4KeiNbq_ZHpiIrlc3HUI-0Op2kK0reu0GU_aem_7uV0b-3b9p1YR6B4GAtAzw

Villalta, 27, gives the Sabres a veteran netminder for the Rochester Americans. He will likely share the net with prospects Topias Leinonen or Scott Ratzlaff, both of whom spent time in the AHL last season.
The 6-foot-3, 190-pound Villalta has spent most of his career in the AHL, compiling 123-83-20 record with a 2.93 goals-against average, a .903 save ... Read the full article

stories.title: 2026 World Cup, Round of 16 Matchday 2: Match thread and discussion
The Blue & Red" scrimmage at the stadium just got switched to season ticket holders only (dammit)
stories.summary:
Hi, I know I might be subjected to verbal abuse for this, but that's OK. I understand message board culture!🙂



	 



	The "Return of the Blue & Red" scrimmage" at the stadium just got switched to season ticket holders only. For those who don't know, it was to a be a scrimmage at the new stadium, with free admission and parking! 
	Ok, there is some sense in it being for season ticket holders.
	They did pay big dollars for season tickets. But they get to go to games at the new stadium 10 times. And the state and county coughed up $850 million for it, and have to do all the upkeep, so well all chipped in. But even so, I wouldn't complain.



	 



	EXCEPT that they didn't think of that ahead of time? Why not? 



	 



	Just 2-3 weeks ago the game was announced and I saw it. You were supposed to come back, right about now, and sign up for the Bills app. Then when tickets went on offer you could try and get a ticket! Even then I figured it as a gamble but I planned to be sitting there the minute it went live and take my shot. Because I wanted to see the new stadium! 



	Like I say, I can understand why they would want to favor season ticket holders. 
	I'm not complaining about that.



	
	I'm complaining because they gave me a letdown disappointed feeling for no reason other than that they couldn't think 3 weeks ahead of time and just announce it that way?



	Grrrr

T McGee’s Quick NHL Draft Recap
stories.summary:
DBTB – 



I’m back from the Draft in scenic BFLO.  A few thoughts that popped into my head during the weekend: 



– If any of you ever get the chance to go to the Draft, you should go.  It is a life-affirming experience.  You’re sitting among the aspiring draftees, their families, friends and coaches, watching people have their dreams fulfilled right in front of you.  Really amazing to be a part of.  And really supportive.  Fans, some of whom have probably never heard of these kids, giving them high-fives and encouraging them.  Really cool.  



– Daxon Rudolph is a guy who I had on my Board at #9.  I think if he can get a little quicker and play with more consistent physicality (probably get stronger), he can be a really good #3.  I’ve watched some more of his shifts since they chose him and there’s one guy he reminds me of..,this will be weird…but I think Sergei Zubov when I watch him.  Everything seems so easy that he doesn’t always feel like he’s trying, he has this crazy knack of just getting pucks through crowds to the net, always moving but never chasing, really clever with manipulating defenders and sees the ice a step ahead. 



– Morozov I had at #21 on my Board, so he went right about where I expected.  I think what’s being missed about this kid are two things: 1) his work ethic is absolutely through the roof, and 2) when you’re the youngest kid in NCAA hockey, on a not-great team, you’re going to have to play conservatively if you want to stay on the ice.  He’s going to have better players next season, they will be a decent team.  I think he’s going to put up bigger numbers and show a lot more offensively in Year 2.  The discussion will change from “probably a 3C, maybe a really good 4C” to “maybe a 2C, a 3C on a really good team”.  He could be a bigger, more physical Anton Lundell. 



– Zellweger is really interesting.  I went back and looked at my 2021 Board, and he was way down at #73.  Probably because he was 5’9.  I don’t know if Ruff can get him to defend the way the coach wants, but he has tremendous wheels, a really good release, and loves to exploit seams in the D (the same way Byram did).  I think he starts as a 3rd pair, and they see how he does in the system.  Everyone went a little coo-coo with Byram’s 11 goals – Zelly had 7 goals and probably played about 5-6 minutes less/game.  Offensively, he won’t be a problem.  Can he get the puck out on time and with possession?  That will be the big question. 



– Murnieks I had at #90.  So great value in my mind for the pick.  The really intriguing thing is early in the year, he was a late 1st rounder.  He started off well, got banged up (I didn’t realize he had multiple concussions this season) and tailed off, and then had a good finish in the U-18s.  He’s a do-everything type of centerman who works his tail off and as a late birthday, will probably get bigger before all is said and done.  He’s probably 4 years away (next year in the Q, 2 years at Boston College, and then maybe a year in the AHL) but could wind up being a really good 4C.  There’s a lot of value in that. 



– Szongoth is an interesting player I had at #158, so kind of freaky that he went at #156.  He’s going to need a lot of time.  There’s some rawness to his game that he gets away with in his age group due to his size and strength.  But he flashes some nice touch offensively, and defensively he’s very methodical and reliable.  I imagine he stays in Finland until he gets a regular shift in Liiga.  But anyone with two first names (Domon Krystzof) who plays for a team named KooKoo is someone you have to take seriously. 



– I was a little disappointed that the Sabres did not add to the current roster at the Draft.  It was all the buzz on the Draft floor, that something big was coming, and it seems they got very close with Winnipeg.  But they didn’t panic and overpay.  Unlike Florida, or Vegas, or LA, the Sabres don’t have a choice but to be a primarily ‘draft and develop’ team.  The city is not a draw, the weather is not a draw, and for most players in their early 20s, the Sabres have been a bottom feeder for their entire life (until last season).  A high pick fell into their laps for Byram, and they used it.  It’s not the perfect outcome, but it’s a pretty smart one nonetheless.

stories.title: Happy 4th of July All!
stories.summary:
Guys: It’s our 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence today, and I am proud to be an American.  God bless you and your families.  I was so proud in Hamburg last year, the town honored my father and Uncle Les for their service during the Korean War.  They hung banners in the town.  I was so proud of my father that day.  He still reminisces his days in the service so long ago.  My dad turns 96 in August, and reminds me how that generation believed service to their country was their honor and priveledge. Every one of my uncles and dad were volunteers. Not one of them in Korea or Vietnam were drafted.



	 



	So God Bless the USA and all of you!

stories.title: Sabres make depth signings on Day One of Free Agency
stories.summary:
No big names to get the fan base excited so far



The Buffalo Sabres were pretty quiet yesterday as NHL Free Agency got underway at noon. 




We have signed forward Conor Sheary and defenseman Dennis Gilbert to one-way contracts worth $850,000. Additionally, we have signed forwards Trevor Kuntar (two years) and Jason Polin (one year) to two-way contracts.Details → https://t.co/PiyYceeGZC— Buffalo Sabres (@BuffaloSabres) July 1, 2026




Two familiar faces to the organization made a return as forward Conor Sheary and defenseman Dennis Gilbert are back, signing NHL veteran minimum $850,000 one-way contracts. 



The pair will have a chance to play for roster spots when training camp begins, but otherwise nothing to really get excited about. 



The Rochester Amerks cupboard is quite bare so the Sabres added Trevor Kuntar and Jason Polin for some veteran leadership. 



It’s hard to tell if there’s more coming, especially with General Manager Jarmo Kekäläinen sounding like he’s done wheeling and dealing. 




"If it’s a perfect fit and also, financially and cap-wise, it fits our system, then we’re open for business.”More quotes from general manager Jarmo Kekäläinen after the start of free agency → https://t.co/2laNxSHQ4l— Buffalo Sabres (@BuffaloSabres) July 1, 2026

stories.title: Sabres trade away Levi to the Oilers
stories.summary:
Buffalo gets a third round draft pick for the goalie prospect



Devon Levi has been traded away by the Buffalo Sabres to the Edmonton Oilers along with a seventh round pick in return for a 2028 third round draft pick. 



After a highly-promising start to the goaltender’s career, he seemed to have hit a rut this last season with the Amerks. 




We have acquired a third-round pick in the 2028 NHL Draft from the Edmonton Oilers in exchange for goaltender Devon Levi and a seventh-round pick in the 2028 NHL Draft.Details → https://t.co/Si8ZSPznST pic.twitter.com/qzKxylF0kw— Buffalo Sabres (@BuffaloSabres) July 1, 2026




Through 120 games as an Amerk, Levi posted 64 wins with 10 shutouts and a 2.52 GAA and .914 SV%.



With the Sabres, Levi had a 3.29 GAA and a .894 SV% in 39 games with 17 games.

stories.title: An uncertainty for the 2026 season in Highmark -- Will the new turf work out?
stories.summary:
We just don't know what to expect from a turf situation. We can make educated guesses, but that's about it.



	 



	The field



	 



	It's an entirely new installation, operated by an inexperienced crew (unless they hired some ringers). I looked at similar fields/climates for comparison:



	 



	
		Pittsburgh: heated field, NFLPA F grade, controversial for being a poor surface in 2025, "borderline unplayable" in response, it was reseeded in 2026
	
	
		Green Bay: heated field, C grade, "slippery and unpredictable"
	
	
		Cleveland: heated field, B grade, I couldn't find many comments on it--maybe that is a good reflection
	



	 



	It seems we want to avoid a Pittsburgh type situation. That would be embarrassing after a $2 billion spending spree. I don't know the specifics in Pitt, but Highmark might have an edge due to what sounds like better technology: "high-tech sub-surface heating and drainage network, UV grow lights, and sub-air ventilation." So, we shall see.



	 



	Injuries



	 



	The common expectation is fewer injuries, or injuries of less severity. It's better on the lower extremities. The concussions are less severe. It seems to me that there would be a decrease in "non-contact injuries" which have been the bane of the Bills. A net plus from a competitive stand point.



	 



	The play



	 



	
		Passing: Grass requires quarterbacks to plant deeper, as the ground can be softer. Deep passes may take a fraction of a second longer to release due to less stable footing
	
	
		Rushing, Receiving, Secondary: Breaks won't be as explosive, cuts won't be as crisp, footwork may need adjustment
	
	
		Kicking: Can be unpredictable; wet weather, divots, and worn-out areas near the hash marks can alter a kicker's plant foot and trajectory. Turf kickers have higher accuracy records. 
	
	
		The lines: "On natural grass, a lineman's first explosive step slightly tears up the turf, which absorbs some of the raw physical impact. Because of this, drive-blocking on dirt or sod requires a slightly wider, lower base to maintain anchor" It's a technique change. I don't expect professional athletes to have a problem coping with that.
	



	 



	It just seems the game may be just a tad slower. But I don't think that will make a competitive difference either way. After all, the same field conditions apply to both teams.



	 



	 



	Overall... if it doesn't become a mud bowl mess in December/January, life is good.

stories.title: Zellweger Signs With the Sabres
stories.summary:
New Defenseman Inks Three Year Contract With Buffalo



Former Anaheim Duck Olen Zellweger has signed a new deal with Buffalo after his trade to Western N.Y. last week on draft day. The 22 year old with three NHL seasons under his belt was a resticted free agent upon arrival. Zellweger’s deal is for three years and about 9 million dollars. (exact numbers to come) He is the top candadate as of now to take Bo Byram’s spot alongside Owen Power.




Olen Zellweger extends in BUF3 years, around $9M for him…nice for both team and player— Elliotte Friedman (@FriedgeHNIC) July 1, 2026




In 2022-23, Zellweger was voted the top defender among players in the OHL, QMJHL and WHL combined. He won the Canadian Hockey League Defenseman of the Year. The speedy Olen scored 22 points in 76 games last year for the Ducks and some are now calling him “Byram Lite”. He has a ways to go to fill Bo’s skates but he has all the tools. This feels like a fair deal for both sides as the Sabres figure out what they have with this player.

stories.title: 2026 NHL Free Agency: Buffalo Sabres signings & Open Thread

Free agency opens today across the NHL at 12pm ET, and the Buffalo Sabres are expected to be active in the market as they look to fill out their roster. We’ve already seen a few players traded & then signed with their new teams, but there are plenty of others out there. 



Use this thread to discuss happenings across the free agency period, including what you hope and think the Buffalo Sabres will do.



We’ll be updating this post with the Sabres signings as well as the NHL free agent tracker as the day goes on.

stories.title: Who are some of your favorite players from over the years???
stories.summary:
I'm a millennial, so my memory only goes back so far, but I'll start with Lee Evans.. I think that if he played in this offense, in his prime, he would be a perennial 1,000yd + receiver.. He played with with terrible QBs throughout his career.. One of the most explosive WRs I think in franchise history, he was a threat to break one every time.. With Josh he would be making the Pro Bowl every year..



	 



	 



	Nate Clements in my mind was the best CB in Bills history.. Many people throw the term "shutdown corner" around but in all actuality it implies to very few players.. Nate Clement was the epitome of a shutdown corner, for the first 5-6 years of his career he was absolutely amazing.. He fell off after he went to San Francisco if I remember, but still, he was pretty much the best corner in the NFL for a solid 5 years straight....



	 



	 



	Other random players: Jason Peters, Travis Henry, Stevie Johnson, Ryan Fitzpatrick, LeSean McCoy, Ted Washington, Pat Williams, Takeo Spikes, London Fletcher, Devin Singletary and so many more..



	 



	 



	 



	Who do you guys got????

stories.title: Stevie Johnson has all that above the shoulders and hopefully Keon catches some of it
stories.summary:
A 7th rounder played on terrible teams who got a big 2nd contract. Many believed Stevie is an over-achiever. But it's clear the guy had it above the shoulders. 



	 



	On the other hand, it speaks volume how little Keon has learned thru his first 2 years. Is it the WR coach or it's Keon himself? We are going to see this season. If Keon does improve and become a reliable target for Allen, I think Stevie Johnson will get a lot of new business as a "mentor." Having the knowledge is one thing. Identifying someone's problem is another. Then the ability getting it to the person is often the most difficult part in coaching/mentoring world.



	 



	I'm also surprised to hear his answer on running the ball. Just another proof I think the guy has it above his shoulders.

stories.title: Red vs Blue Scrimmage Tickets ???
stories.summary:
Has anyone heard when these are going to be made available for the season ticket holders yet?  
	 



	Also, has anyone heard if the season ticket holders don’t want theirs, if they’ll be available to the public?   
	 



	I need one more, as I’m trying to bring my gf.  We already have four from our seasons.   Don’t care where the seat is, as I’ll probably spend the entire time walking around and taking pictures. Just need entry.

stories.title: Sabres ink Malenstyn to new long-term deal
stories.summary:
Malengoat stays! Free agency week started with the Buffalo Sabres locking up a key piece of the bottom six to a new six-year deal with Beck Malenstyn signing a new contract for $17.5 million, which works out to an AAV of $2.9167M. 



As a key piece of the Sabres fourth line, the fast-skating hard-hitting forward featured in 81 regular season games, scoring 7 goals and 7 assists averaging 11:15 a night with 38 PIM and a 40% faceoff win percentage. He added to that with 1 goal and 2 assists in 13 playoff games playing 10:30 a game. 



The new deal marks more than double his previous contract at 2x $1.35M, but it’s a well-earned raise given how essential he has been to the Sabres forward corps. 




MALENGOAT 🐐We have signed forward Beck Malenstyn to a six-year contract with an AAV of $2.9167 million.Details → https://t.co/REPC5llWvF pic.twitter.com/8B3rbt02GB— Buffalo Sabres (@BuffaloSabres) June 29, 2026




With Malenstyn now locked up, an integral part of the fourth line is in place along with Sam Carrick and the Sabres can focus on the top nine.

stories.title: When did the 12th Man become obsolete in Bills fandom?
stories.summary:
Interesting chat on WGR550 this morning about how not only will OJ not be getting his name in the ring of honor but doesn't sound like the 12th Man is getting one either.  Then they talked about does that even matter and they both agreed no not really.  They went on to talk about how nobody ever refers to Bills fans as the 12th Man anymore and Seattle took that over in the NFL with the 12s.



	I would say that's all true, I couldn't tell you the last time I remember anybody bringing up the 12th Man but it is kinda funny when you think about what a big deal that was in the 90s and into the 2000s.



	 



	The obvious answer to my question is it was replaced by Bills Mafia, I know that.  But did it happen overnight or was it just a thing that was gradually phased out into oblivion. I feel like in the early days of Bills Mafia the 12th Man was also still a thing?  Not sure if Seattle's rise with it's Super Bowl team and all of the hype they got around calling their fans the 12s also stole some of that shine from the Bills 12th Man days.

stories.title: Buffalo Sabres Open Thread, Wk of 6/29
stories.summary:
The NHL Draft has come and gone and the Buffalo Sabres have certainly been busy retooling the roster given there were some expiring contracts and players entering pivotal final seasons of their current deals. 



This week will see the opening of NHL Free Agency, starting at 12:00 p.m. ET on Wednesday July 1. Under the terms of the Collective Bargaining Agreement, any contact or contract negotiation between NHL teams and pending unrestricted free agents (UFAs) is strictly prohibited prior to the official opening, even though every year we see prior discussions all the time.



Between contract extensions, early trades, NMCs and NTCs, this has led to a pretty weak free agent market, but that should not dissuade aggressive General Managers from making moves. We’ll have a tracker going here on DBTB to update the latest signings on Wednesday.

stories.title: If you weren't thrilled about the Brady hire as HC do you feel any better about it now?
stories.summary:
Well it was 5 months ago yesterday he was announced as HC.



	 



	 



	When he got hired I'll admit my reaction was very negative.  I think off the emotion of the Broncos loss, the messy press conference over the firing of McDermott, and my feeling was at the time the Bills didn't really interview a lot of candidates.  



	 



	 



	While I'm still not sold about the hire my feeling months removed from it all is that I'm willing to roll with him as HC.

stories.title: Day Two Draft Recap For the Sabres
stories.summary:
Buffalo was significantly quieter on day two of the draft. Due to their various trades, they only picked three times in rounds 2-7. There were over 100 selections between their picks as they selected Morozov at 20th overall and their next pick was at 124th overall. Whether it was intentional or just how the board fell, Buffalo added forward prospects with all three of their selections.



Round 4 Pick 124 – Olivers Murnieks – Center – 6’1” – (7-13-20 in 31 games in QMJHL)



Murnieks had a disappointing season offensively due to injuries but is an intriguing prospect. He is a high energy player who projects to be a bottom six forward in the NHL. His defensive reliability and motor set him apart from his peers. The Latvian native is committed to Boston College and plans to join the squad in the Fall. He’s young for his draft class as he turns 18 at the end of July. Between major junior and college, he’ll have plenty of opportunities to grow into a quality pro player.



Round 5 Pick 156 – Doman Szongoth – Center – 6’1” – (10-18-28 in 44 games in U20 SM-sarja)



Buffalo continued their run on centerman by selecting Szongoth in the 5th round. He is just the fifth Hungarian drafted by an NHL team. If he were to make the Sabres roster in the future, he would be the first of his countrymen to make their NHL debut. He is a talented two-way center that will need time to grow into his game. He is expected to return to the top junior league in Finland but could jump to the men’s league if he takes an additional step in development.



Round 6 Pick 188 – Dylan Dumont – Right Wing – 6’0” – (28-16-44 in 62 games in QMJHL)



Dumont is a scoring winger who attacks the center of the ice. He’s another young player for his draft (mid-August birthday) who hit his growth spirts in the last couple of years with room to continue to develop physically. He was his team’s leading goal scorer last year and is expected to be leaned on for goal production again next season. His style of play makes him one to keep an eye on as he rounds out his game.



Despite the lack of draft capital, Buffalo was able to add to their already impressive stockpile of young talent. Time will tell whether these prospects can make any noise at the NHL level. Fans will get their first look at them at prospect camp in the next few days at Harborcenter.

stories.title: 2026 NHL Draft – How To Watch, Draft Order & Rounds 2-7 Open Thread
stories.summary:
With the 2026 NHL Draft being held in Buffalo, there was much anticipation about what new General Manager Jarmo Kekäläinen would do, and if you are a fan of activity, you certainly got some. 



The Sabres picked up 2022-23 Canadian Hockey League Defenseman of the Year Olen Zellweger from the Anaheim Ducks for forward Anton Wahlberg and a second-round pick in the 2026 NHL Draft (just acquired from Chicago). 



They followed that up with the selections of offensive-minded defenseman Daxon Rudolph at #4, and Russian center Ilia Morozov at #20, both high floor prospects even if they are not big swings for the fences. 



The Sabres currently hold these remaining picks:




Round 4, 124th overall



Round 5, 156th overall



Round 6, 188th overall




Jarmo’s Day 1 Recap



“I was fortunate that I always worked with a general manager that didn’t stick his fingers in the process,” Kekäläinen said about the eleven previous drafts he had overseen while in scouting. 



He took the same approach during his first draft as general manager of the Sabres, trusting the years-long scouting process conducted by assistant GM Jerry Forton and his staff as they crafted the list that led to them making two first-round selections.



“We explored all the options, and I contacted all the other teams in the league to make sure that they know that all our options are open. Nothing was attractive enough for us to do anything else than pick.”



While there was heavy interest in the fourth pick, Kekäläinen said it had become clear by Friday morning that there were no packages worthy of trading away a piece that could become a cornerstone of the pipeline, leading to the Sabres keeping the pick and selecting defenseman Rudolph. 



“(Zellweger) He’s just getting started. He’s playing 17 minutes a game. We always say that defensemen need about 200 games before you can really see what they become. He’s not there yet, so I think he’s just growing as a defenseman right now.”



Kekäläinen said he sees Zellweger potentially competing with fellow newcomer Louis Crevier (a 6-foot-8 defenseman acquired in the Byram deal) to play as Owen Power’s partner next season.



Second Chance for Wheeling and Dealing



Kekäläinen needs some free time at today’s 2nd day of the draft to make some deals, he will certainly have it. The Buffalo Sabres moved their 2nd round pick from Chicago yesterday for defenseman Olen Zellweger and the team has no 3rd rounder either. Hopefully Jarmo can concentrate on trade talk with his fellow GMs with his first hour or two void of picks. Improving the team that will take the ice this fall is imperative.



Jedi Analysis 



As far as draft needs go, I’m thinking the Sabres should grab two more forwards for organizational depth. The Rochester Americans have really become depleted up front the last two years. Between player graduation to the NHL and others being traded out, our AHL affiliate is really short on quality forwards. 



From there, the team should probably grab a goalie. It just makes sense to draft one every year.I think it is safe to say that the goal today and in the coming weeks is to add a big talent to the roster among the forward ranks. Can Jarmo pull it off? In my opinion, he better.



Enjoy the festivities today. The selections will come fast and furious now, unlike the dragged out spectacle of yesterday’s picks. Added bonus, no Justin Bieber. (hopefully)



How to Watch



Rounds Two to Seven



Date: Saturday, June 27th 2026



Start time: 11:00 AM EST



TV: NHL Network (US), Sportsnet & TVA Sports (CAN)



Online stream: ESPN+

stories.title: The View from Patriots Wire
stories.summary:
Came across this article:
	
	“The Patriots have seemingly pulled away in the race with the many moves they've made in the offseason.



	 



	Meanwhile, the Bills haven't done much of anything to signal they'll be any better than they were last year. One could even argue that they'll take a step back after firing head coach Sean McDermott. Josh Allen is Superman on the football field, but he's going to need more than D.J. Moore and a middle-of-the-road defense to fly high enough to reach his first Super Bowl….



	 



	The Bills have to be seeing the moves being made by the Patriots, and it would be hard to imagine them feeling good about their offseason in relation to the competition. They are now faced with a situation where the Patriots have one of the best young quarterbacks in the league in Drake Maye and a top-10 defensive unit that gave Allen problems last season.



	 



	Will the Bills ever wake up and realize fighting fire with fire is much better than laying the hopes and dreams of an entire franchise on the shoulders of one man?”



	 



	Do you think this is justified or comically arrogant?

stories.title: If There Was a First Song to be Played at the New Stadium What Song Would You Like it to Be ?
stories.summary:
So the Bills will be opening their new home Highmark Stadium this season & I was wondering what song would you all think should be fitting for the very first jam on their brand new Kick A** sound system with the Mafia on hand ? 



	 



	Of course one of the all time favorite sayings that is bound to be used is the Marvism "Where would you rather be than right here - Right now" So I thought if they could incorporate this song with that Marvism it would be fitting .



	 



	I know we have had other posts in the past of songs that we felt should be played when the team comes on the field & such but this one I would like to keep just a bit different ! Lets keep this one to something to do about a new start, a new home, Starting over, something of that sort . But it's got to rock, no slushy love song type songs  this is the Bills new home so lets start it by blowing the top of the place .



	 



	I love the intro to this song which could be playing in the back ground with the stadium lights doing some cool type of visual thing to match the piano riff while someone was doing a introduction of sorts like "where else would you rather be than Right here (then BANG the lyrics & fans come in) - RIGHT NOW !!! Jam on !!



	 



	Then of course at the end fire works  all around the canopy !! 



	 



	So give out some ideas as far as the song but remember keep it on the subject of something new as I said in the previous sentence ... 



	 



	What say you Mafia ???
